title: OpenClaw should not crash when residual subagent output arrives after the active run ends

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62793.

task.summary: Diagnose why residual subagent output can crash the gateway after the active run ends.
task.instructions: Use the subagent incident evidence to confirm the late-output crash, localize it to missing active-run guards around listener updates, and propose an ops-level repair plan that safely discards residual output.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Residual subagent stdout and stderr can arrive after a run ends and crash the gateway with `Agent listener invoked outside active run`.
error_logs: subagent residual output triggered Unhandled promise rejection
Gateway crash interrupted all active sessions
reproduction_steps: Inspect the residual-output evidence from subagent runs.
Explain where the listener lifecycle should drop updates once the run is over.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models listener-runtime incident triage around residual subagent output.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic residual-output incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62793.
